五十四年前，NASA发射了Surveyor 2，这是一项无任务的探索月球表面的任务。 course，航天器在经过一次不正确的航向校正燃烧后进入了一次跌落路线，并以每秒2.7公里的速度撞入月球表面。但是，在发射过程中使用的火箭助推器沿着不同的轨迹进入太空，现在已经开始绕地球轨道飞行。这就是一直在研究2020 SO的天文学家的结论，今年8月首次发现了一个不寻常的物体。
 NASA的Surveyor程序旨在演示在月球上发射，与之通信，控制其以及将其降落的可行性，从而为以后的载人飞行铺平道路。 1966年5月30日发射的Surveyor 1获得了巨大成功，轻松实现了其主要目标，同时还返回了许多月球表面图像和关键工程数据。因此，NASA希望在第二次飞行任务中取得成功，就寄予厚望。目的是在其前身以东的月球上测量2号测量师，以证明倾斜进近和着陆的可行性。
 经过一系列小小的延误后，Surveyor 2于1966年9月20日美国东部标准时间7：31：59.8成功发射。运载火箭是使用液态氢和液态氧作为推进剂的Atlas-Centaur火箭。一旦半人马座的引擎关闭，航天器滑行了66秒钟，验船师2离开了用过的半人马座。火箭将其剩余的推进剂驱逐出去，以设定其自身与Surveyor 2的安全距离，从而使其轨迹偏离月球约5675公里，并将其送入太阳轨道。天文学家已经确认那颗半人马火箭是2020年。
 对于Surveyor 2来说，一切似乎都进行得很顺利。它朝月球的轨迹几乎完全对准了目标，发射后16.5小时只需稍加燃烧即可进行航向校正。那是当事情变得非常糟糕的时候。飞船的游标引擎3未能与其他引擎一起点燃，从而使Surveyor 2在太空中翻滚。在接受失败之前，美国宇航局的科学家试图使引擎启动39次。就是这样，当他们试图在9月23日“勘测员2”坠入月球表面（哥白尼山的东南部）之前，通过一系列工程测试可以收集到哪些有用数据。
    尽管MON-10氧化剂的流量问题似乎是罪魁祸首，但NASA从未正式确定发动机未能点火的原因。在NASA将重点转移到乘员探索月球之前，又进行了五次Surveyor任务，其中四次成功。
 同时，用完的半人马座火箭继续沿太空飞驰，几乎被人们遗忘了-至少直到今年8月，当时夏威夷的天文学家使用全景观测望远镜和快速反应系统（Pan-STARRS）在太空中发现了一个神秘物体。即将通过地球的太阳轨道。 Pan-STARRS天文台是探测快速移动物体（例如新小行星）的关键工具。但是这个被称为2020 SO的新天体的移动速度太慢而无法成为小行星，而且它的轨道也很奇怪。
    管理着JPL近地物体研究中心的保罗·乔达斯（Paul Chodas）是许多渴望了解2020 SO的科学家之一。他对它的近乎圆形的路径进行了计算机模拟，并指出它的轨道相对于地球几乎没有倾斜。对此，乔达斯很感兴趣，他以相反的方式进行了模拟，发现该物体在1966年9月足够接近地球，以至于起源于地球。换句话说，2020 SO可能是发射了注定的Surveyor 2的废旧Centaur火箭，特别是考虑到其轨道略微异常。这证明了该物体被太阳发出的光子推动，因此2020 SO必须相对较大且质量较低，就像一个空的火箭助推器一样。 （相比之下，岩石小行星相对较小且较大。） 
Chodas向他的世界各地的天文学家发送了电子邮件警报，因此每个人都可以帮助追踪2020 SO＆＃8482;在接下来的几个月中，随着它越来越靠近地球。后续的光谱学观察可以深入了解该物体的​​组成，并且光谱数据与1960年代用于制造Centaur火箭助推器的不锈钢一致。尽管该数据中的细微差异可以用暴露于恶劣的太空天气54年后的钢材风化来解释，但该假说还不是灌篮。
 ＆＃34;我们知道，如果我们想将苹果与苹果进行比较，我们需要尝试从已经进入地球轨道多年的另一架半人马座火箭助推器获取光谱数据，然后才能确定它是否更适合2020年SO频谱，亚利桑那大学的Vishnu Reddy说，他领导了进行关键后续观察的团队。 ＆＃34;由于绕地球的半人马座助推器以极高的速度在天空中飞行，我们知道很难锁定IRTF [NASA红外望远镜设施]足够长的时间来获得可靠的数据集。＆＃34;
  2020年11月被地球引力捕获，并于12月1日距地球27,400英里。那时，雷迪和他在IRTF的同事能够捕获另一架半人马D火箭助推器的红外光谱-这次是从1971年发射的通信卫星开始的。当他们将该光谱与大约2020 SO收集的数据进行比较时，光谱匹配。 2020 SO也是半人马座火箭的助推器，很可能是用于Surveyor 2任务的助推器。
 2020 SO现在正在远离地球，应该在几个月内逃脱地球引力，届时它将遵循新的太阳轨道。但是天文学家们希望它能在2036年重返地球，而当它发生时，他们将准备学习更多。
note_en:
Fifty-four years ago, NASA launched the  Surveyor 2, an uncrewed mission to explore the surface of the Moon. Alas, the spacecraft went into a tumble en route after a failed course-correction burn, and it slammed into the lunar surface at 2.7 kilometers per second. But the rocket booster used during its launch followed a different trajectory into space and has now  begun orbiting the Earth. That's  the conclusion of astronomers who have been studying 2020 SO, an unusual object first spotted this past August.
 NASA's Surveyor program was designed to demonstrate the feasibility of launching, communicating with, controlling, and landing an uncrewed spacecraft on the Moon, thereby paving the way for later crewed missions.  Surveyor 1, launched on May 30, 1966, was a resounding success, easily meeting its primary objectives, while also returning many images of the lunar surface and critical engineering data. So NASA's hopes were high for building on that success with the second mission. The intent was to  land Surveyor 2 on the Moon just east of its predecessor to demonstrate the feasibility of an oblique approach and landing.
 After a series of minor delays,  Surveyor 2 launched successfully on September 20, 1966, at 7:31:59.8 EST. The launch vehicle was an Atlas-Centaur rocket using liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen as propellants. Once the Centaur's engines were shut down and the spacecraft had coasted for 66 seconds, Surveyor 2 departed from spent Centaur. The rocket expelled its residual propellants to set a safe distance between itself and the Surveyor 2, giving it a trajectory that would miss the Moon by a good 5,675 kilometers and send the rocket into a solar orbit. That Centaur rocket is what astronomers have confirmed to be 2020 SO.
 Everything seemed to be going smoothly for Surveyor 2. Its trajectory toward the Moon was almost perfectly on target, with just a light burn needed for a course correction 16.5 hours after launch. That's when things went horribly wrong. The craft's Vernier Engine 3 failed to ignite along with the others, sending Surveyor 2 tumbling through space. NASA scientists tried to get the engine started 39 times before accepting defeat. That's when they tried to glean what useful data they could from a series of engineering tests before Surveyor 2 crashed into the lunar surface on September 23, southeast of the crater Copernicus.
    NASA never officially determined the cause of the engine's failure to ignite, although an issue with the flow of MON-10 oxidizer seems to be the culprit. There were five more Surveyor missions, four of them successful, before NASA switched its focus to crewed exploration of the Moon.
 Meanwhile, the spent Centaur rocket continued on its merry way through space and was pretty much forgotten—at least until August of this year, when astronomers using the Panoramic Survey Telescope and Rapid Response System (Pan-STARRS) in Hawaii spotted a mysterious object in a solar orbit that was due to pass close by the Earth. The Pan-STARRS observatory is a key instrument in detecting fast-moving objects like new asteroids. But this new object, dubbed 2020 SO, was moving too slowly to be an asteroid, and it also had a rather strange orbit.
    Paul Chodas, who manages JPL's Center for Near Earth Object Studies, was  among the many scientists eager to learn more about 2020 SO. He ran computer simulations of its nearly circular path, noting that its orbit was barely tilted relative to Earth. Intrigued, Chodas ran the simulation in reverse and found that the object passed close enough to Earth in September 1966 to have originated from there. In other words, 2020 SO could be the spent Centaur rocket that launched the doomed Surveyor 2, particularly given its slightly anomalous orbit. That is evidence that the object is being pushed around by the photons emitted by the Sun, so 2020 SO would have to be relatively large and have low mass, just like an empty rocket booster. (Rocky asteroids, in comparison, are relatively small and massive.)
    Chodas sent out an email alert to his fellow astronomers around the world, so everyone could help track 2020 SO's progress over the next few months as it moved closer and closer to Earth. Follow-up spectroscopy observations yielded insight into the object's composition, and the spectrum data was consistent with the stainless steel used to manufacture Centaur rocket boosters in the 1960s. But the hypothesis wasn't yet a slam dunk, although the small discrepancies in the data could be explained by the weathering of the steel after 54 years of exposure to harsh space weather.
 "We knew that if we wanted to compare apples to apples, we'd need to try to get spectral data from another Centaur rocket booster that had been in Earth orbit for many years to then see if it better matched 2020 SO's spectrum,"  said Vishnu Reddy of the University of Arizona, who headed the team that performed the critical follow-up observations. "Because of the extreme speed at which Earth-orbiting Centaur boosters travel across the sky, we knew it would be extremely difficult to lock on with the IRTF [NASA Infrared Telescope Facility] long enough to get a solid and reliable data set."
  2020 SO was captured by Earth's gravity in November and came within 27,400 miles from Earth on December 1. That's when Reddy and his colleagues at the IRTF were able to capture the infrared spectrum of another Centaur D rocket booster—this time from a 1971 launch of a communication satellite. When they compared that spectrum to the data gathered about 2020 SO, the spectra matched. 2020 SO is also a Centaur rocket booster, most likely the one used for the Surveyor 2 mission.
 2020 SO is now moving away from the Earth and should escape Earth's gravity within a few months, at which point it will follow a new solar orbit. But astronomers expect it to return to Earth in 2036, and they will be ready to learn even more when it does.
title: 被地球重新捕获的奇怪物体是来自Surveyor 2的1966年助推火箭
title_en: Strange object recaptured by Earth is 1966 booster rocket from Surveyor 2

note:
副总统迈克·彭斯（Mike Pence）将宣布一个由18名宇航员组成的干部，美国宇航局很可能会从中选拔将作为阿耳emi弥斯计划的一部分前往月球的指挥官，飞行员和任务专家。
 多个消息来源称，便士将在周三于佛罗里达州举行的美国国家太空委员会会议上发布名单，作为NASA的Artemis Moon计划更新的一部分。这些不是即将执行的任务的正式机组人员任务，而是将从中选拔宇航员进行即将到来的飞行的干部。一些宇航员将出席。
 第一个载人登月任务Artemis II可能会携带四名宇航员绕月飞行，以测试“猎户座”飞船的生命支持系统。这实质上是对1968年12月进行的阿波罗8号任务的重现，它可能会在2023年开始飞行。第一个登月任务Artemis III可能会搭载两到四名宇航员，不太可能在2026年之前飞行。将取决于即将上任的拜登政府对Artemis的支持程度。
  美国航空航天局（NASA）打算宣布此“阿耳emi弥斯干部” 2020年早些时候，但COVID-19禁止举行盛大的公共仪式，其目的是引起人们对Artemis的兴奋，并获得公众的支持。然后，今年秋天，该机构不想在大选前宣布姓名，因为担心该计划会被政治化。
 尽管如此，鉴于彭斯将在他作为国家太空委员会主席的最后一次会议上宣布这一消息，仍可能难以避免。本次会议的议程表明，这将是一个胜利的时刻。让特朗普政府注意到其在民用和军事领域的成就。
 总部位于休斯敦的宇航员办公室对此发表声明一直存在抵制。最初要求干部包括姓名时，最初是反对宇航员的，但白宫和美国宇航局总部却要求输入姓名。休斯顿的宇航员领导层不希望有一个公开命名的团体，因为它实际上会创建一个“收藏夹”团体。在办公室内部，削弱了太空飞行人员之间的团结感。美国宇航局的官员拒绝对此事发表评论。
  目前办公室中有47位符合飞行任务资格的NASA宇航员，其中包括2017级的11名尚未飞入太空的成员。在这些军团中，有16名宇航员是女性，而在干部中被任命的军人无疑将受到严格的审查，因为其中一个可能成为第一个踏上月球的妇女。 
此公告的时间很明显。当然，如果NASA计划在三年内将人类送入深空，那么现在是时候让宇航员部队中的一个小组开始训练了，无论是研究地质知识还是让他们沉浸在各种月球着陆器开发计划中。
 但是，一些消息人士质疑为什么现在需要宣布这一消息，而便士离任仅一个多月时间才离开国家航天局。该机构的行政长官吉姆·布莱登斯汀（Jim Bridenstine）也计划在2021年1月20日辞去特朗普总统任职的最后一天。太空委员会会议因此为特朗普政府提供了最后一次盖章的机会。在Artemis计划上。
 ＆＃34;有人在出门的路上指派机组人员是不寻常的，＆＃34;一位前宇航员告诉Ars。 ＆＃34;最终可能会引起轰动，因为他们希望这样做，但这确实没有任何意义。宇航员办公室讨厌被用作政治道具，这实在令人讨厌。＆＃34;
 通常，飞行任务由宇航员办公室主任（现任三届宇航员帕特里克·福雷斯特）和约翰逊航天中心飞行运营局的领导来处理。太空理事会会议在美国东部时间下午12:30（世界标准时间17:30）开始，并将通过NASA电视流播。
note_en:
Vice President Mike Pence will announce a cadre of 18 astronauts from whom NASA is likely to chose the commanders, pilots, and mission specialists who will go to the Moon as part of the Artemis Program.
 Multiple sources said Pence would release a list of names on Wednesday at the National Space Council meeting in Florida as part of an update on NASA's  Artemis Moon program. These will not be formal crew assignments for upcoming missions but, rather, a cadre from which astronauts will be selected for upcoming flights. Some of the astronauts will be in attendance.
 The first crewed lunar mission, Artemis II, is likely to carry four astronauts around the Moon to test the Orion spacecraft's life-support systems. This will essentially be a reprise of the Apollo 8 mission flown in December 1968, and it could fly as soon as 2023. The first Moon landing mission, Artemis III, may carry two or four astronauts, and is unlikely to fly before 2026. This will depend on the extent to which the incoming Biden administration supports Artemis.
  NASA had intended to announce this "Artemis Cadre" earlier in 2020, but COVID-19 precluded public ceremonies with pomp—the goal was to generate excitement about Artemis, and engender public support. Then, this fall, the agency did not want to announce the names near the election, for fear of politicizing the program.
 Still, it may be difficult to avoid that given that Pence is making the announcement at what will be his final meeting as chairman of the National Space Council.  The agenda of this meeting suggests it will be something of a "victory lap" for the Trump administration to note its accomplishments in civil and military space.
 There has been some resistance to making this announcement from the Houston-based Astronaut Office. When initially asked for names to include in a cadre, the Astronaut Office at first demurred, but the White House and NASA headquarters pressed for names. The Houston-based leadership of the astronaut corps did not want a publicly named group because it would essentially create a group of "favorites" within the office, undermining a sense of unity shared among the space fliers. Officials from NASA declined comment for this story.
  There are presently  47 NASA astronauts in the office who are eligible for a flight assignment, including the 11 members of the 2017 class who have not yet flown into space. Among the corps, 16 of the astronauts are women, and those named among the cadre will undoubtedly be subjected to heightened scrutiny as one of them may become the first woman to set foot on the Moon.
 The timing of this announcement is notable. Certainly, if NASA is planning to send humans into deep space within three years, it is time for a group within the astronaut corps to begin training, be it studying geology or immersing themselves in the various lunar lander development programs.
 However, some sources questioned why this announcement needed to be made now, with only a little more than a month before Pence would leave office, and exit the National Space Council. The agency's administrator, Jim Bridenstine, too, plans to resign on President Trump's last day in office, on January 20, 2021. The space council meeting thus offers the Trump administration its last chance to put its stamp on the Artemis program.
 "It is unusual for someone to assign a crew on their way out the door," one former astronaut told Ars. "This will probably end up making a splash because they want it to, but it really doesn't mean anything. The Astronaut Office hates being used as a political prop, and this reeks of it."
 Typically, flight assignments are handled by the chief of the Astronaut Office—currently three-time astronaut Patrick Forrester—and the leadership of the Flight Operations Directorate at Johnson Space Center. The space council meeting begins at 12:30pm ET (17:30 UTC) and will be  streamed on NASA TV.
title: 副总统迈克·彭斯（Mike Pence）被任命为Artemis宇航员干部
title_en: Vice President Mike Pence is set to name a cadre of Artemis astronauts

频闪的灯光会诱发一些容易产生癫痫发作的人的癫痫发作，但这并没有阻止许多引人注目的游戏加入效果。通常在启动时会出现样板警告，说这是有可能的，但是在大多数游戏中，更多的警告是可能会出现这种类型的闪光灯，例如，如果有几个闪光灯互相接连爆炸。许多游戏还提供了降低闪烁强度或改变外观的选项，以及其他辅助功能选项。
 “ Cyber​​punk”似乎从根本上占据了特别危险的地带，因为其游戏世界充满了那种肮脏的，闪烁的霓虹灯照明，并伴有肮脏的，未来派的反乌托邦。但是在游戏开始的几个小时内，发生了一场更为严重，设计周到的事件，已经使Game Informer的审阅者感到癫痫发作。它涉及（否则非常有趣的）“大脑失灵”或BD，通过戴上专用的耳机……它可以在强烈的闪光灯下启动，让您的角色重现他人录制的体验：
  当“适合” BD（尤其是Judy）时，V将获得旨在使该实例发作的头戴式耳机。该头戴式耳机可套在双眼上，并具有快速闪烁的红色和白色闪烁的LED，就像神经病学家在现实生活中使用实际设备触发癫痫发作以进行诊断时那样。如果不是根据IRL设计进行建模，那将是非常偶然的巧合，因此，这是我个人建议您完全避免的一个方面。当您注意到耳机在起作用时，请完全移开视线或闭上眼睛。这是一种设计成触发癫痫发作的灯光模式，这在我自己的个人游戏中非常有用。
 您可以看到上面的屏幕截图中提到的事件（此后发生，但是您可以看到设备）。我从自己的演奏中很清楚地回忆起这一刻，并记得当时确实是一场激烈的灯光秀。不幸的是，这个人引起了严重的发作，并且在第10次发布后可能会对其他许多人造成严重影响。
 在更改“ Cyber​​punk 2077”外观的众多选择中，没有一个可以减少我能找到的闪烁的灯光。我已经问过CD Projekt Red这件事，希望他们能在发布时或发布时发布一些东西来减轻问题。该公司确实在Twitter上表示正在寻找解决方案。
note_en:
Strobing lights can induce seizures in some people prone to them, but that hasn’t stopped many high-profile games from including them for effect. Usually there is a boilerplate warning on boot saying this is a possibility, but in most games it’s more of a warning that there may potentially be flashing lights of this type, for example if several flashbang grenades went off one after the other. Many games also offer an option to reduce the intensity of flashing lights or otherwise change their appearance, along with other options for accessibility.
 “Cyberpunk” seems to tread especially dangerous territory fundamentally, as its game world is full of the kind of seedy, flickering-neon lighting one associates with a grimy, futuristic dystopia. But within the first few hours of the game there is a much more severe and thoughtlessly designed event that has already  caused a reviewer at Game Informer to experience a seizure. It involves the (otherwise quite interesting) “braindances,” or BDs, which let your character relive experiences recorded by others, by donning a special headset… that boots up with intense flashing lights:
  When “suiting up” for a BD, especially with Judy, V will be given a headset that is meant to onset the instance. The headset fits over both eyes and features a rapid onslaught of white and red blinking LEDs, much like the actual device neurologists use in real life to trigger a seizure when they need to trigger one for diagnosis purposes. If not modeled off of the IRL design, it’s a very spot-on coincidence, and because of that this is one aspect that I would personally advise you to avoid altogether. When you notice the headset come into play, look away completely or close your eyes. This is a pattern of lights  designed to trigger an epileptic episode and it very much did that in my own personal playthrough.
 You can see the event referred to in the screenshot above (taken afterwards, but you can see the device). I recall this moment quite clearly from my own playthrough, and remember thinking it was rather an intense light show indeed. Unfortunately for this person, it caused a serious episode and could do so for many others upon its release on the 10th.
 Among the many options for changing the appearance of “Cyberpunk 2077,” there isn’t one for reducing flashing lights that I could find. I’ve asked CD Projekt Red about this and hopefully they can ship something to mitigate the issue at or near launch. The company did  say on Twitter that it was looking into a solution.
title: 赛博朋克2077在没有警告或缓解的情况下对诱发癫痫发作的顺序提出了批评
title_en: Cyberpunk 2077 draws criticism for seizure-inducing sequence with no warning or mitigation
